title: Sort Important Papers
summary: Keep important papers and files where you can find them.
learnerPromise: Keep important papers and files where you can find them.
whyItMatters: Losing documents causes stress and delays. An organized system means you can find what you need faster.
realLifeUses: Keep medical records and insurance papers sorted.
Store ID documents like passport and birth certificate safely.
Back up important files to the cloud.
youWillLearn: Use labeled folders for physical papers.
Create a simple folder system on your computer.
miniChallenge: Gather 5 important papers and sort them into labeled piles or folders.
steps: Gather all your important documents in one place.
Sort them into categories: personal, medical, financial, school, work.
Put each category into a labeled folder or envelope.
Create matching folders on your computer or cloud drive.
Scan or photograph the most important papers and save them digitally.
completionCriteria: All physical documents are in labeled folders.
You have at least one digital backup of your most important document.
commonProblems: Piling papers in a drawer and forgetting what is there.
Not having digital copies if originals are lost.
Creating too many categories and getting confused.
tips: Use broad categories. Five folders is better than twenty.
Take a photo of your ID and insurance card on your phone.
Review your documents once a year and throw out what you no longer need.
